title: calylophus serrulata
intro: Calylophus serrulata, also known as the pink desert marigold, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Calylophus serrulata has been used to promote health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Calylophus serrulata, including teas and infusions, have been used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
